title: Trousdale Modern
description: Editorial coverage of a contemporary glass-and-steel residence on the Trousdale ridge. Floor-to-ceiling glazing, a cantilevered volume over the canyon, and a material palette of raw concrete and warm walnut — documented at first light.

title: Los Feliz Craftsman
description: A 1922 Craftsman bungalow restored to its original bones. Wide-plank oak floors, hand-set tile, and a garden that took twelve years to grow — photographed to honour the time built into every surface.

title: Malibu Ridge
description: A ridgeline compound above the Pacific. Three structures connected by stone paths, a pool that disappears into the horizon, and light that changes every twenty minutes. Shot over two days to catch it all.

title: Silver Lake Compound
description: A mid-century duplex compound reimagined as a single-family residence. Original terrazzo, a new addition by a local architect, and a rooftop deck with an unobstructed view of the reservoir.

title: Bel Air Estate
description: A gated estate on two acres with a guest house, motor court, and a formal garden designed in the 1960s. The main residence was photographed room by room across a single golden afternoon.

title: Hancock Park Georgian
description: A Georgian Colonial on one of the park's most storied streets. Original millwork, a library with floor-to-ceiling shelves, and a back garden with a brick path that leads nowhere in particular.
